tools.name: navigate_to
tools.description: Get step-by-step walking directions to a destination using clock-position directional cues (e.g. 'at 2 o'clock, 50 meters'). Includes slope warnings for steep grades. Always use this when the user asks how to get somewhere. INVOCATION CONDITIONS: Call ONLY after user explicitly confirmed a destination.

tools.name: get_location_info
tools.description: Get information about the user's current location including address and nearby points of interest with accessibility details. Use when the user asks 'where am I?' or wants to know what's around them.

tools.name: nearby_search
tools.description: Search for nearby places like restaurants, cafes, pharmacies, bus stops, etc. Returns accessibility information (wheelchair access) for each place. Use when the user asks to find a specific type of place nearby. INVOCATION CONDITIONS: Call ONCE per search. Do NOT also call maps_query.

tools.name: reverse_geocode
tools.description: Get a human-readable address from GPS coordinates. Use when you need to describe the user's location.

tools.name: get_walking_directions
tools.description: Get walking directions between two named locations (addresses or place names). Includes slope warnings. Use when GPS coordinates are not available.

tools.name: preview_destination
tools.description: Preview a destination using Street View imagery before arrival. Returns a scene description with safety warnings and navigation cues. Use when the user asks 'what does it look like there?' or before starting navigation to an unfamiliar place.

tools.name: validate_address
tools.description: Validate and correct a spoken or typed address. Fixes common speech-to-text errors (e.g. 'one two three main street' → '123 Main St'). Use before navigating to a user-spoken address to ensure accuracy. Returns corrected address with coordinates.

tools.name: google_search
tools.description: Search the web for current information. Use for verifying brand or product names, looking up business hours, menus, prices, news, events, or any factual question that needs up-to-date information. Behavior: WHEN_IDLE — results are delivered after the model finishes speaking.

tools.name: identify_person
tools.description: Acknowledge that face recognition runs automatically in the background. Do NOT call this tool — face results are injected into your context automatically as [FACE ID] entries. If called, returns a no-op confirmation.

tools.name: resolve_plus_code
tools.description: Decode a Google Plus Code (e.g. '849VQJQ5+JQ') to GPS coordinates. Plus Codes are short alphanumeric codes that represent a location. Use when the user provides a Plus Code. Behavior: WHEN_IDLE.

tools.name: convert_to_plus_code
tools.description: Convert the user's current GPS coordinates to a Google Plus Code. Use when the user asks for a location code or wants to share their precise position. Behavior: WHEN_IDLE.

tools.name: get_accessibility_info
tools.description: Query nearby accessibility features from OpenStreetMap: tactile paving, wheelchair ramps, audio traffic signals, pedestrian crossings, stairs, handrails, and sidewalk surface quality. Use when the user asks about accessibility of their surroundings or when navigating in unfamiliar areas. Behavior: WHEN_IDLE.

tools.name: maps_query
tools.description: Query Google Maps for detailed place information, reviews, ratings, business hours, and geographic reasoning. Use for open-ended location questions like 'What good restaurants are nearby?', 'Is there an accessible pharmacy open now?', 'What are the reviews for this place?'. Different from nearby_search — this provides richer, more conversational answers with reviews and details. Behavior: WHEN_IDLE. INVOCATION CONDITIONS: Call ONCE per question. Do NOT also call nearby_search.

tools.name: extract_text_from_camera
tools.description: Read and extract text from the current camera view. Use when the user asks 'what does it say?', 'read this for me', 'any text here?', 'what's written there?', or similar text-reading requests. Do NOT call this proactively — only when the user explicitly requests text reading. Safety-critical text (danger signs, warnings) is detected automatically.

data.accessibility_note: Walking route. Watch for crosswalks and intersections. Listen for traffic signals at crossings.

tools.name: navigate_to
tools.description: Get step-by-step walking directions to a destination using clock-position directional cues (e.g. 'at 2 o'clock, 50 meters'). Includes slope warnings for steep grades. Always use this when the user asks how to get somewhere. INVOCATION CONDITIONS: Call ONLY after user explicitly confirmed a destination.

tools.name: get_location_info
tools.description: Get information about the user's current location including address and nearby points of interest with accessibility details. Use when the user asks 'where am I?' or wants to know what's around them.

tools.name: nearby_search
tools.description: Search for nearby places like restaurants, cafes, pharmacies, bus stops, etc. Returns accessibility information (wheelchair access) for each place. Use when the user asks to find a specific type of place nearby. INVOCATION CONDITIONS: Call ONCE per search. Do NOT also call maps_query.

tools.name: reverse_geocode
tools.description: Get a human-readable address from GPS coordinates. Use when you need to describe the user's location.

tools.name: get_walking_directions
tools.description: Get walking directions between two named locations (addresses or place names). Includes slope warnings. Use when GPS coordinates are not available.

tools.name: preview_destination
tools.description: Preview a destination using Street View imagery before arrival. Returns a scene description with safety warnings and navigation cues. Use when the user asks 'what does it look like there?' or before starting navigation to an unfamiliar place.

tools.name: validate_address
tools.description: Validate and correct a spoken or typed address. Fixes common speech-to-text errors (e.g. 'one two three main street' → '123 Main St'). Use before navigating to a user-spoken address to ensure accuracy. Returns corrected address with coordinates.

tools.name: google_search
tools.description: Search the web for current information. Use for verifying brand or product names, looking up business hours, menus, prices, news, events, or any factual question that needs up-to-date information. Behavior: WHEN_IDLE — results are delivered after the model finishes speaking.

tools.name: identify_person
tools.description: Acknowledge that face recognition runs automatically in the background. Do NOT call this tool — face results are injected into your context automatically as [FACE ID] entries. If called, returns a no-op confirmation.

tools.name: resolve_plus_code
tools.description: Decode a Google Plus Code (e.g. '849VQJQ5+JQ') to GPS coordinates. Plus Codes are short alphanumeric codes that represent a location. Use when the user provides a Plus Code. Behavior: WHEN_IDLE.

tools.name: convert_to_plus_code
tools.description: Convert the user's current GPS coordinates to a Google Plus Code. Use when the user asks for a location code or wants to share their precise position. Behavior: WHEN_IDLE.

tools.name: get_accessibility_info
tools.description: Query nearby accessibility features from OpenStreetMap: tactile paving, wheelchair ramps, audio traffic signals, pedestrian crossings, stairs, handrails, and sidewalk surface quality. Use when the user asks about accessibility of their surroundings or when navigating in unfamiliar areas. Behavior: WHEN_IDLE.

tools.name: maps_query
tools.description: Query Google Maps for detailed place information, reviews, ratings, business hours, and geographic reasoning. Use for open-ended location questions like 'What good restaurants are nearby?', 'Is there an accessible pharmacy open now?', 'What are the reviews for this place?'. Different from nearby_search — this provides richer, more conversational answers with reviews and details. Behavior: WHEN_IDLE. INVOCATION CONDITIONS: Call ONCE per question. Do NOT also call nearby_search.

tools.name: extract_text_from_camera
tools.description: Read and extract text from the current camera view. Use when the user asks 'what does it say?', 'read this for me', 'any text here?', 'what's written there?', or similar text-reading requests. Do NOT call this proactively — only when the user explicitly requests text reading. Safety-critical text (danger signs, warnings) is detected automatically.

data.answer:
You are currently at the heart of Union Square, a vibrant and iconic public plaza and surrounding neighborhood in San Francisco. It is a well-known historical landmark, bustling with activity and offering a diverse range of experiences.

Union Square is renowned for its upscale shops, including major department stores and luxury boutiques, as well as numerous restaurants, hotels, and a lively nightlife. The plaza itself serves as a hub for various events, such as art shows, performances, and even ice skating during certain seasons.

The area is very accessible, with wheelchair-accessible entrances, seating, and parking options available. You'll find convenient paid parking lots, garages, and both free and paid street parking.

Nearby landmarks and establishments include Macy's, the Grand Hyatt San Francisco, and multiple Starbucks Coffee Company locations. The SFMTA - Union Square Garage is also located beside the square. Union Square is considered a family-friendly destination.

tools.name: navigate_to
tools.description: Get step-by-step walking directions to a destination using clock-position directional cues (e.g. 'at 2 o'clock, 50 meters'). Includes slope warnings for steep grades. Always use this when the user asks how to get somewhere. INVOCATION CONDITIONS: Call ONLY after user explicitly confirmed a destination.

tools.name: get_location_info
tools.description: Get information about the user's current location including address and nearby points of interest with accessibility details. Use when the user asks 'where am I?' or wants to know what's around them.

tools.name: nearby_search
tools.description: Search for nearby places like restaurants, cafes, pharmacies, bus stops, etc. Returns accessibility information (wheelchair access) for each place. Use when the user asks to find a specific type of place nearby. INVOCATION CONDITIONS: Call ONCE per search. Do NOT also call maps_query.

tools.name: reverse_geocode
tools.description: Get a human-readable address from GPS coordinates. Use when you need to describe the user's location.

tools.name: get_walking_directions
tools.description: Get walking directions between two named locations (addresses or place names). Includes slope warnings. Use when GPS coordinates are not available.

tools.name: preview_destination
tools.description: Preview a destination using Street View imagery before arrival. Returns a scene description with safety warnings and navigation cues. Use when the user asks 'what does it look like there?' or before starting navigation to an unfamiliar place.

tools.name: validate_address
tools.description: Validate and correct a spoken or typed address. Fixes common speech-to-text errors (e.g. 'one two three main street' → '123 Main St'). Use before navigating to a user-spoken address to ensure accuracy. Returns corrected address with coordinates.

tools.name: google_search
tools.description: Search the web for current information. Use for verifying brand or product names, looking up business hours, menus, prices, news, events, or any factual question that needs up-to-date information. Behavior: WHEN_IDLE — results are delivered after the model finishes speaking.

tools.name: identify_person
tools.description: Acknowledge that face recognition runs automatically in the background. Do NOT call this tool — face results are injected into your context automatically as [FACE ID] entries. If called, returns a no-op confirmation.

tools.name: resolve_plus_code
tools.description: Decode a Google Plus Code (e.g. '849VQJQ5+JQ') to GPS coordinates. Plus Codes are short alphanumeric codes that represent a location. Use when the user provides a Plus Code. Behavior: WHEN_IDLE.

tools.name: convert_to_plus_code
tools.description: Convert the user's current GPS coordinates to a Google Plus Code. Use when the user asks for a location code or wants to share their precise position. Behavior: WHEN_IDLE.

tools.name: get_accessibility_info
tools.description: Query nearby accessibility features from OpenStreetMap: tactile paving, wheelchair ramps, audio traffic signals, pedestrian crossings, stairs, handrails, and sidewalk surface quality. Use when the user asks about accessibility of their surroundings or when navigating in unfamiliar areas. Behavior: WHEN_IDLE.

tools.name: maps_query
tools.description: Query Google Maps for detailed place information, reviews, ratings, business hours, and geographic reasoning. Use for open-ended location questions like 'What good restaurants are nearby?', 'Is there an accessible pharmacy open now?', 'What are the reviews for this place?'. Different from nearby_search — this provides richer, more conversational answers with reviews and details. Behavior: WHEN_IDLE. INVOCATION CONDITIONS: Call ONCE per question. Do NOT also call nearby_search.

tools.name: extract_text_from_camera
tools.description: Read and extract text from the current camera view. Use when the user asks 'what does it say?', 'read this for me', 'any text here?', 'what's written there?', or similar text-reading requests. Do NOT call this proactively — only when the user explicitly requests text reading. Safety-critical text (danger signs, warnings) is detected automatically.
